name: B V M S N
description_short: B&V Credit Union is a Kansas-based, member-owned not-for-profit credit union established in 1954, serving employees of Black & Veatch and their families with competitive loan rates and personalized financial services.
description_long:
B&V Credit Union was founded on June 15, 1954, by Black & Veatch employees seeking to provide convenient and personalized financial services to fellow employees and their families. Starting with just 66 members and $2,051 in shares, the credit union has grown while maintaining its founding principle of "service and convenience" through member-driven governance. The institution operates as a separate entity from Black & Veatch Corporation, though Black & Veatch serves as a valued sponsor with no financial interest in the credit union. B&V CU is regulated by the State of Kansas and holds memberships with the Credit Union National Association (CUNA), Kansas Credit Union Association (KCUA), and is NCUA-insured.

B&V Credit Union offers auto loans (new vehicles at 5.25-7.00% APR, used at 6.79-7.19% APR), signature loans at 13.00% APR, share accounts, share certificates, calculators, and e-services including online banking and bill payment through their Virtual Branch. The credit union provides access to personalized financial counseling and membership benefits typical of credit unions, with a minimum $5.00 deposit to open an account. They maintain physical offices in Overland Park, Kansas, with phone, toll-free, fax, and email support.

As a member-owned, not-for-profit institution, B&V CU distinguishes itself by returning profits to members through favorable interest rates rather than enriching external shareholders. Each member holds equal voting power regardless of account balance, and the Board of Directors and Supervisory Committee are elected volunteers from the membership serving three-year terms without compensation. This governance structure contrasts sharply with for-profit banks and provides members direct influence over institutional direction and priorities.

B&V Credit Union's primary limitation is restrictive membership eligibility—only Black & Veatch employees and their immediate family members (spouse, partner, children, parents, grandparents, siblings) qualify. This employer-sponsored model, while creating strong community alignment, excludes the general public from membership. Additionally, the website provides limited detail on savings account rates, CD offerings, and the full range of loan products available, making comprehensive rate comparison difficult.
services: Member-directed governance through annual meetings and board elections
pros: Competitive auto loan rates (5.25-7.00% for new, 6.79-7.19% for used vehicles)
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure returns profits to members rather than shareholders
Equal voting power for all members regardless of account balance
Volunteer Board of Directors with no compensation ensures member interests are prioritized
Established 70+ years (founded 1954) with demonstrated financial stability and commitment to member service
NCUA-insured deposits and Kansas state regulation provide consumer protection
Virtual Branch and online e-services for convenient account access and payments
Family members of Black & Veatch employees eligible for membership, extending access beyond direct employees
cons: Membership is restricted to Black & Veatch employees and their families only—general public cannot join
Limited transparency on website regarding savings account rates, CD yields, and complete loan product offerings
Signature loan rate of 13.00% APR is relatively high compared to typical credit union personal loans
Single physical office location in Overland Park, Kansas may limit convenience for geographically dispersed members
Website lacks information on credit-building products, overdraft protection, or lines of credit availability
best_for: Black & Veatch employees seeking affordable auto financing through a trusted, member-owned institution
Family members of Black & Veatch employees looking for personalized financial services and competitive rates
Members prioritizing not-for-profit governance and democratic control over institutional decision-making
Individuals in the Kansas area seeking an alternative to traditional banks with lower-cost membership ($5 minimum)
diagnosis: B&V Credit Union is best for Black & Veatch employees and their families seeking competitive auto loans and member-owned financial services with democratic governance. The critical caveat is membership exclusivity—public access is prohibited, making this institution unavailable to the general population regardless of creditworthiness or financial need.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.

meta_description: B V M S N — NCUA-insured credit union in Walnutport, PA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
